unresolved: pkg/tsdb/jaeger/utils/client_utils.go:TransformSearchResponse and pkg/tsdb/jaeger/utils/grpc_utils.go:TransformGrpcSearchResponse both have unguarded [0] accesses in their sort.Slice comparators (lines 51-52 and 53-54 respectively), but these are triggered by non-empty responses containing traces with zero spans — a distinct input scenario from the empty result set described in the issue. They may warrant a follow-up fix but are not required to fix the described panic.

pkg/tsdb/jaeger/utils/client_utils.go:TransformSearchResponse and pkg/tsdb/jaeger/utils/grpc_utils.go:TransformGrpcSearchResponse each contain a sort.Slice comparator that accesses Spans[0] (and ScopeSpans[0].Spans[0]) without a length guard. These are latent panics triggered when a search response contains traces with empty Spans arrays — a different edge case from the empty-result-set scenario described in the issue — so they are not required to change to fix the reported bug, but are closely related.
